mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which directly translates to higher consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which significantly raises the cost of capital and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.2% over 3 months, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with moderate, stable consumer price inflation indicates healthy consumer demand and pricing power without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, fostering an environment for positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows positive growth and CPIAUCSL monthly change is between 0.1% and 0.3%, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market health, indicating an impending or ongoing economic contraction that severely impacts corporate profitability and leads to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such triggered instances during periods of economic contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month expansion in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and mining activity, reflecting strong underlying economic demand and supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5% for three consecutive months, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of housing starts reflect strong underlying demand for housing and associated goods, indicating robust consumer and business confidence and future economic activity, which generally translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST remains above its 12-month moving average, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is already elevated and consumer price inflation continues to accelerate, it signals persistent inflationary pressures requiring further aggressive monetary tightening, which severely dampens economic growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods immediately following a recession, strong quarterly GDP growth signals a broad economic rebound and recovery, leading to increased corporate profits and renewed investor optimism, which drives significant positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows quarterly growth above 2.0% (annualized) during a post-recession period, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such observations.
